review_comments:
["`dispose` triggers an async IPC call but the returned promise is neither awaited nor handled. If the remote connection is gone (common during shutdown), this can surface as an unhandled rejection. Consider making this fire-and-forget explicitly and handling errors (e.g., log at warn level) to avoid noisy unhandled promise rejections.
```suggestion
					void channel.call('disposeGateway', info.gatewayId).catch(error => {
						this._logService.warn(`[McpGateway][Workbench] Failed to dispose remote gateway: ${info.gatewayId}`, error);
					});
```", "`dispose` triggers an async IPC call but the returned promise is neither awaited nor handled. If the remote connection is already closed (likely in browser/serverless scenarios), this can become an unhandled rejection. Consider explicitly handling/logging errors to avoid noisy unhandled promise rejections.
```suggestion
					void channel.call('disposeGateway', info.gatewayId).then(undefined, error => {
						this._logService.warn(`[McpGateway][BrowserWorkbench] Failed to dispose remote gateway: ${info.gatewayId}`, error);
					});
```", "This change introduces propagation of `chatSessionResource` into the tool call context, but the existing unit tests for `McpGatewayToolBrokerChannel` don't assert that the context is forwarded/parsed correctly. Please add/update a test that calls `callToolForServer` with `chatSessionResource` and verifies `tool.call` receives `context.chatSessionResource` as the expected `URI`.", 'I would like to 
* make this optional as a first step, 
* next adopt this API in chat extension
* finally make this a mandatory argument (I fail to see why it would ever be optional)

I believe the code would be cleaner if its mandatory and this step by step implementation would make it easy to adopt as well.', 'Once the UI is mandatory, we can remove the extra comments  `enabling inline elicitation UI in the chat response.`', "## Pull request overview

Adds an optional `chatSessionResource` URI that can be threaded from the proposed `vscode.lm.startMcpGateway(...)` API down through the gateway IPC path so MCP tool calls can be associated with a chat session (enabling chat-inline elicitation UI instead of fallbacks).

**Changes:**
- Extend the proposed API `lm.startMcpGateway` to accept an optional `chatSessionResource?: Uri`.
- Plumb `chatSessionResource` through ext host → main thread → workbench gateway creation and into the MCP tool broker `callToolForServer` context.
- Update gateway channel callers to pass the new argument when creating gateways (local + remote).

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| src/vscode-dts/vscode.proposed.mcpServerDefinitions.d.ts | Adds optional `chatSessionResource` parameter to proposed `lm.startMcpGateway` API docs/signature. |
| src/vs/workbench/contrib/mcp/electron-browser/mcpGatewayService.ts | Accepts `chatSessionResource` and forwards it when creating local/remote gateways via IPC. |
| src/vs/workbench/contrib/mcp/common/mcpGatewayToolBrokerChannel.ts | Receives `chatSessionResource` per tool call, parses it, and forwards it in the tool call context. |
| src/vs/workbench/contrib/mcp/common/mcpGatewayService.ts | Updates `IWorkbenchMcpGatewayService.createGateway` signature and docs to include `chatSessionResource`. |
| src/vs/workbench/contrib/mcp/browser/mcpGatewayService.ts | Forwards `chatSessionResource` when creating remote gateways in browser scenarios. |
| src/vs/workbench/api/common/extHostMcp.ts | Ext host `startMcpGateway` accepts optional `chatSessionResource` and serializes it to main thread. |
| src/vs/workbench/api/common/extHost.protocol.ts | Ext host protocol updated: `$startMcpGateway(chatSessionResource?: UriComponents)`. |
| src/vs/workbench/api/common/extHost.api.impl.ts | Exposes updated `lm.startMcpGateway(chatSessionResource?)` in the API factory. |
| src/vs/workbench/api/browser/mainThreadMcp.ts | Revives optional `chatSessionResource` and passes it to `IWorkbenchMcpGatewayService.createGateway`. |
| src/vs/platform/mcp/node/mcpGatewayChannel.ts | Thread `chatSessionResource` through gateway creation into broker `callToolForServer` IPC calls. |
</details>

## Copilot's findings




- **Files reviewed:** 9/10 changed files
- **Comments generated:** 3


"]

review_comments:
['The Run button label/title are user-facing strings but are not localized (and include emoji). Please externalize these strings via `nls.localize(...)` and keep them consistent with existing UI labels.
```suggestion
		const runButtonLabel = nls.localize('runPython', "Run Python");
		const runButton = dom.$('button.monaco-button.primary', {
			type: 'button',
			title: runButtonLabel
		});
		runButton.textContent = runButtonLabel;
```', 'This temp file path is hardcoded to `/tmp`, which won’t work on Windows and may not be appropriate on all platforms. Please use a cross-platform temp location and proper quoting/escaping for paths.
```suggestion
		// Create terminal and execute the script
		this.commandService.executeCommand('workbench.action.terminal.new');

		// Hide command completely, only show clean output
		setTimeout(() => {
			// Clear screen and execute silently without relying on a hardcoded temp path
			const cleanCommand = `clear\necho "🐍 Running Python..."\npython3 - <<'EOF' 2>&1\n${code}\nEOF`;
```', 'Building a multi-command shell script (heredoc + `clear` + `python3`) and injecting arbitrary editor text into it is fragile (e.g. code containing `EOF`) and platform-specific. Consider invoking the terminal in a safer/cross-platform way rather than constructing a heredoc script string.', 'The "Save Content" action currently logs the full editor contents to the devtools console, which can leak user content and doesn’t provide any actual save behavior. Implement a real save flow or remove/disable this command until it’s functional.
```suggestion

```', 'This sends the selected image (base64) to a hardcoded external endpoint over plain HTTP. That’s a major security/privacy risk for product code. Please remove this or make the endpoint configurable, require explicit user consent, and use HTTPS.', 'The payload includes the full image encoded as base64. Before sending user files off-device, this flow should be gated behind explicit user consent and clear UX about what data is transmitted and to where.', '`relativePath` is taken from the remote response without validation. If it contains `..` segments or absolute paths, it can be used to write outside the intended output folder. Please validate/sanitize the path before using it.', 'Because `URI.joinPath` normalizes `..` segments, `URI.joinPath(targetRoot, relativePath)` can escape `targetRoot`. Ensure the resulting URI is within `targetRoot` (e.g. `extUri.isEqualOrParent`) before calling `writeFile`.', 'This notification message is user-facing but not localized. Please use `nls.localize(...)`/`localize2` for strings shown via `INotificationService`.', 'The error notification message is user-facing but not localized (and uses string concatenation). Please localize it and use placeholders rather than concatenation for translatable strings.', 'This CSP change allows the workbench to connect to a specific external HTTP origin, which weakens the security model for all users. Please remove hardcoded external endpoints from CSP and avoid enabling plain HTTP in production workbench CSP.
```suggestion

```', 'This CSP change allows the dev workbench to connect to a specific external HTTP origin. Please avoid hardcoding external endpoints in CSP; prefer HTTPS and gate dev-only endpoints behind configuration/build flags.
```suggestion

```', '`extensionAllowedProposedApi` appears to be an extension-level manifest setting, but it is added to the repo root `package.json` and isn’t referenced elsewhere. If needed, it should live in the relevant extension’s `package.json`; otherwise remove it to avoid carrying an unknown/unused top-level field.
```suggestion

```', 'Dependencies are updated here, but `package-lock.json` is not updated in this PR. Please update the lockfile alongside dependency version changes to keep installs reproducible and CI passing.', 'Changing `electron` from a pinned version to a caret range can introduce unintended upgrades and break reproducible builds. VS Code typically pins Electron exactly; consider keeping an exact version (and updating the lockfile).
```suggestion
    "electron": "41.1.1",
```', 'Changing `gulp` to a caret range (and especially a major version) is high-risk for the build pipeline. If this is intentional, ensure the change is accompanied by required code updates and a lockfile update; otherwise keep the pinned version.
```suggestion
    "gulp": "5.0.1",
```', '## Pull request overview

This PR introduces two new workbench contributions (“Monaco Input” and “Bonian Agent”), wires them into the workbench entrypoint, and includes supporting UI/docs plus several build/runtime configuration changes.

**Changes:**
- Adds a new **Monaco Input** auxiliary-bar view with an embedded Monaco editor and a “Run Python” action.
- Adds a new **Bonian Agent** sidebar view + controller that posts selected images to a vision-to-code endpoint and writes generated files into the workspace.
- Updates **CSP**, **dependencies**, and **build compilation plumbing**, and adds large markdown tutorials.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 16 out of 17 changed files in this pull request and generated 16 comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| src/vs/workbench/workbench.common.main.ts | Registers the new Monaco Input and Bonian Agent contributions in the workbench startup imports. |
| src/vs/workbench/contrib/welcomeGettingStarted/common/gettingStartedContent.ts | Modifies Getting Started titles/walkthrough title strings. |
| src/vs/workbench/contrib/monacoInput/browser/monacoInputView.ts | Implements the Monaco Input view pane and terminal-driven Python execution. |
| src/vs/workbench/contrib/monacoInput/browser/monacoInput.contribution.ts | Registers the Monaco Input view container/view and title-bar actions. |
| src/vs/workbench/contrib/monacoInput/browser/media/monacoInput.css | Adds styling for the Monaco Input view. |
| src/vs/workbench/contrib/bonianAgent/browser/bonianAgentViewPane.ts | Implements a Bonian Agent view using an overlay webview for pipeline status UI. |
| src/vs/workbench/contrib/bonianAgent/browser/bonianAgentController.ts | Adds the vision-to-code request + generated-project file writing logic. |
| src/vs/workbench/contrib/bonianAgent/browser/bonianAgent.contribution.ts | Registers the Bonian Agent view container/view and Explorer context action. |
| src/vs/platform/utilityProcess/electron-main/utilityProcess.ts | Extends crash “reason” union type with `memory-eviction`. |
| src/vs/code/electron-browser/workbench/workbench.html | Changes workbench CSP `connect-src` to allow a specific external HTTP origin. |
| src/vs/code/electron-browser/workbench/workbench-dev.html | Same CSP `connect-src` change for dev workbench. |
| package.json | Adds a top-level proposed-API allowlist field and updates multiple dependency versions/ranges. |
| MONACO_INPUT_TUTORIAL.md | Adds a long-form tutorial document for Monaco Input. |
| MONACO_INPUT_STEP_BY_STEP_TUTORIAL.md | Adds a detailed step-by-step tutorial document for Monaco Input. |
| build/lib/compilation.ts | Changes gulp source stream options (adds `encoding: false` cast). |
| build/lib/compilation.js | Compiled output corresponding to the compilation.ts change. |
</details>

review_comments:
['The walkthrough title was changed to a hard-coded string and the localization call was commented out. This introduces an unlocalized, branded title in a core walkthrough; please restore `localize(...)` and avoid product renaming in core content.
```suggestion
		title: localize('gettingStarted.setup.title', "Get started with VS Code"),
```', 'New source files in this repo are expected to include the standard Microsoft copyright header at the top. `monacoInputView.ts` is missing it; please add the header to match other workbench sources.', 'The Run button label/tooltip are user-visible but not localized (and include an emoji). Please externalize these strings via `nls.localize(...)` (and consider avoiding emoji in core UI labels/tooltips).
```suggestion
			title: nls.localize('runPythonCode', "Run Python Code")
		});
		runButton.textContent = nls.localize('runPython', "Run Python");
```', '`runPython` builds a shell script using Unix-specific paths/commands (`/tmp`, `clear`, `cat`, `rm`) and relies on a fixed timeout before sending to the terminal. review_comments:
['In listTunnels(), `seen` tracks tunnel IDs (`seen.add(info.tunnelId)`), but the additional-tunnel loop checks `seen.has(tunnelName)`. Since `tunnelName` is a label/name (not an ID), this check won’t prevent duplicates and can cause the same tunnel to be added twice when it’s found in both passes. Consider checking de-duplication using the fetched tunnel’s `tunnelId` (after lookup) and/or tracking both IDs and names explicitly.', 'The running-server cleanup guard is currently a no-op: the `if r.commit == commit_prefix || ...` block is empty and `*running = None` always executes. If a newer server is started while this background task for an older process is still draining output, it can incorrectly clear `running` for the newer server. Consider only clearing `running` when it still refers to the same process (e.g., compare a stored PID/UUID, or store the commit in a local and check it before setting `None`).
```suggestion
					*running = None;
				}
			}
```', 'This configuration schema example suggests `"tunnel:abc123"` is a valid `chat.remoteAgentHosts[].address`, but `parseRemoteAgentHostInput` only accepts ws(s)/http(s) URLs and `WebSocketClientTransport` would turn `tunnel:abc123` into `ws://tunnel:abc123` (invalid). Unless there is separate parsing/transport support for tunnel addresses here, this description is misleading; consider removing the tunnel example or documenting tunnels via the dedicated tunnel flow/settings instead.
```suggestion
					address: { type: 'string', description: nls.localize('chat.remoteAgentHosts.address', "The address of the remote agent host (e.g. \"localhost:3000\" or \"ws://127.0.0.1:8081\").") },
```', 'This comment says the sessions provider is created synchronously during `addSSHConnection`’s `onDidChangeConnections` event, but tunnel providers are created by `TunnelAgentHostContribution` when the tunnel is cached (via `onDidChangeTunnels`/`_reconcileProviders`). Consider updating the comment to match the actual provider creation mechanism so future changes don’t rely on the wrong ordering assumption.', 'The `authenticate` request handler throws `ProtocolError(-32602, ...)` when the token is rejected. `-32602` is the JSON-RPC “Invalid params” code, but an auth failure seems better represented as `AHP_AUTH_REQUIRED` (`-32007`) (which is already used elsewhere) or another protocol-specific auth error code. Using `-32602` may cause clients to mis-handle auth failures as caller bugs instead of prompting to sign in.', 'The updated test no longer verifies the semantics of `authenticate` beyond “response has a result”. Since the typed protocol defines `authenticate` success as an empty object, it would be stronger to assert the expected shape (e.g. `deepStrictEqual(result, {})`) and/or that no error is returned, so regressions don’t silently pass.
```suggestion
		const resp = await responsePromise as { result?: Record<string, unknown>; error?: { code: number; message: string; data?: unknown } };

		assert.ok(!resp.error);
		assert.deepStrictEqual(resp.result, {});
```', 'Tunnel relay connections in the shared process are never explicitly torn down on normal disconnect/dispose. `TunnelRelayTransport` doesn’t override `dispose()` to call `ITunnelAgentHostMainService.disconnect(connectionId)`, and `TunnelAgentHostService.disconnect()` only removes the remote entry. This can leave the shared-process `TunnelConnection` (and underlying dev tunnel relay/WebSocket) running indefinitely after the user disconnects or the renderer reloads. Consider adding a `dispose()` implementation on `TunnelRelayTransport` (or another lifecycle hook) that invokes `disconnect(this._connectionId)` best-effort.', '`TunnelRelayTransport` is a new transport implementation but there’s no accompanying unit test coverage similar to `SSHRelayTransport` (see `src/vs/platform/agentHost/test/electron-browser/sshRelayTransport.test.ts`). Adding a small test suite for message filtering by connectionId, malformed JSON dropping, onClose behavior, and send() forwarding would help prevent regressions.', "## Pull request overview

- Introduces tunnel-backed remote agent host connections end-to-end (CLI tunnel port, shared process tunnel relay service, Sessions UI/actions).
- Refactors auth flow to use `IAgentInfo.protectedResources` and updates client state wiring (shared `SessionClientState`).
- Removes/updates legacy agent host APIs and adjusts tests accordingly.

['`requestNeedsInput` is set from the view model here, but it’s never cleared when `setModel(undefined)` runs or when the view model is disposed. This can leave the context key stuck `true`, which can incorrectly keep the Cancel/stop UI and keybindings enabled for a widget with no active session. Consider explicitly setting both `requestInProgress` and `requestNeedsInput` back to `false` in the `!model` branch and in the `onDidDisposeModel` handler.', 'The name `requestInProgressOrPendingToolCall` is now misleading because the expression also includes `ChatContextKeys.requestNeedsInput` (which isn’t a “pending tool call”). Renaming this constant to match its broadened meaning (or splitting the conditions) would avoid confusion for future maintenance.', "## Pull request overview

This PR addresses the missing “Stop/Cancel” button scenario in Chat by introducing a new context key that represents when a request is paused and needs user input, and then using that context to make cancellation available in more states.

**Changes:**
- Added a new `ChatContextKeys.requestNeedsInput` context key.
- Bound and updated `requestNeedsInput` in `ChatWidget` based on the chat model’s `requestNeedsInput` observable.
- Expanded cancel/stop action context expressions to treat “needs input” similarly to “in progress” for relevant UI/keybindings.

<details open>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| ---- | ----------- |

| src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/widget/chatWidget.ts | Wires the model’s `requestNeedsInput` into the widget’s context keys. |
| src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/actions/chatExecuteActions.ts | Updates cancel/stop gating expressions to account for `requestNeedsInput`. |
</details>

[{'sha': '70bb47bc61f95b1be7de6c6941f31b9159bf4e94', 'message': "fix: exempt agent instructions from workspace subpath filter

AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, and copilot-instructions.md live at the workspace
root by design. The CLI harness restricts items to workspaceSubpaths
(.github, .copilot, .agents, .claude) which filtered out these root-level
agent instruction files. Now items with groupKey 'agent-instructions' are
exempt from the subpath filter, matching the existing exemption for
instructionFileFilter patterns."}, {'sha': '446d7308520822a6592b56b988a32e7feb2e0a0e', 'message': 'sessions: include AGENTS.md in instructions listing

The sessions AI customization tree view and overview were only calling
listPromptFiles(PromptsType.instructions) to discover instruction files.
However, AGENTS.md (along with CLAUDE.md and copilot-instructions.md)
is classified as an agent type by getPromptFileType(), so it was never
returned by that call.

Fix by also calling listAgentInstructions() and merging the results
(deduplicating by URI), matching the pattern already used by the
workbench customization editor.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>'}]
